title: Divide and Conquer Recurrence (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if the closed form is wrong, base the expo implementation marks on whether the student applies exponentiation by squaring correctly using their own formula.
This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. The Java method is calc(long n); the Python function is calc(n). Both are correct.
The recurrence a_{n+1} = 2*a_n + 1 with a_0 = 0 has the closed form a_n = 2^n - 1. The implementation computes 2^n mod (10^9+7) using exponentiation by squaring, then subtracts 1.

rubric_points.name: Closed form derivation
rubric_points.target: The student identifies that a_n = 2^n - 1, so calc(n) = (2^n mod c) - 1 where c = 10^9 + 7.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No closed form is used; the student attempts to iterate the recurrence n times (O(n)), or returns a constant unrelated to 2^n.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: The student uses exponentiation by squaring on base 2 but with a wrong constant term (e.g., returns 2^n + 1 or 2^n without subtracting 1), or uses the wrong base (e.g., computes n^2 instead of 2^n).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: The student correctly identifies a_n = 2^n - 1 and implements calc(n) as expo(2, n, 10^9+7) - 1 (or equivalent).

rubric_points.name: Expo base case: b == 0
rubric_points.target: When the exponent b == 0, the expo function returns 1 (or 1 % c).

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No base case is present; the recursion never terminates or returns an incorrect constant for b == 0.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: A base case is present but uses b == 1 instead of b == 0, causing an off-by-one and incorrect results for n = 0.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns 1 (or 1 % c) when b == 0, correctly terminating the recursion.

rubric_points.name: Expo odd case: reduce by 1 and multiply
rubric_points.target: When b is odd, returns (a * expo(a, b-1, c)) % c.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: The odd case is absent, or does not reduce b by 1, or does not multiply by a.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Reduces b by 1 and recurses, but does not multiply by a, or does not apply % c.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns (a * expo(a, b-1, c)) % c when b is odd.

rubric_points.name: Expo even case: halve and square
rubric_points.target: When b is even, computes tmp = expo(a, b//2, c) once and returns (tmp * tmp) % c.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: The even case is absent, or does not halve b, or does not square the recursive result.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Halves b and recurses, but squares incorrectly (e.g., calls expo twice with b//2 doubling the work to O(b)), or does not apply % c to the result.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Calls expo(a, b//2, c) exactly once, stores the result, then returns (tmp * tmp) % c, achieving O(log b) depth.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: calc(n) returns (2^n - 1) mod (10^9+7) correctly for all valid inputs, without using forbidden keywords.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Returns incorrect results for most inputs, or contains forbidden keywords ('import', '**', 'pow', etc.).

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Returns correct results for small inputs but fails for large n (e.g., due to integer overflow from missing modulo steps, or O(n) iteration timing out), or the -1 adjustment is missing.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Returns (2^n - 1) mod (10^9+7) correctly for all valid inputs in O(log n); forbidden keywords are absent; modulo is applied at each recursive step.

buckets.label: Internally consistent but incorrect final result
buckets.description: Logic follows consistently from the student's own closed form and expo implementation, but the result is incorrect due to a systematic error (e.g., missing -1, or wrong modulus constant).
